author: garyrong
content:
Hey guys, I have a proposal to move the account-creation state gas **out of intrinsic gas**.
  
Currently, for a contract-creation transaction, the intrinsic gas unconditionally pre-charges the account-creation state gas, and refunds it if the creation fails. The issue with this mechanism is that it assumes the destination never exists beforehand, which is not always true: the target address may have been pre-funded in which case no account creation actually happens.

I propose to remove the account-creation state gas from intrinsic gas, and only charge it when an account creation actually happens. Combined with my previous proposal for the Create/Create2/Call* opcodes, the spec around account-creation state gas charging becomes:
  
- Creation tx, Create, Create2: charge the account-creation state gas when the account is actually created (the destination does not **exist** beforehand).
- Normal tx, Call*: charge the account-creation state gas when a non-zero value transfer targets an **empty** recipient.
- In all cases above, the charged state gas is refunded if the frame fails in the end.

Since the ordering between the state gas charge and a potential exceptional halt matters:
  
- For Creation tx, Create, Create2, the charge happens after the collision check and before the init code execution.
- For Normal tx, Call*, the charge happens after the sanity checks (call depth, balance, etc.) and before the value transfer.

This gives a single uniform rule at all depths: no special-casing of the top-level frame, and no refund path in intrinsic gas.

author: draganrakita
content: Catching up to the chat, was on vacation last few days, and previous week was a cowork.

author: draganrakita
content: It felt strange if we move it at the end of the call and then caller needs to do a math and leave necesarry gas for this.

author: draganrakita
content: My thinking at that point of time was to not discard the gas, gas imo should represent the amount of cpu/state used with halt we are getting empty gas. But i didn't take into consideration that this removes a edge cases on where state gas is spend, this should be good enought reason to not do refill on halt (but just on REVERT)

author: draganrakita
content: Yeah i have i64 `state_gas_spend` and u64 `reservoir` that is tracking the EIP-8037 [here](https://github.com/bluealloy/revm/blob/f1554a6cf0ed8fd25f716eb6eec2a4bbdba9fa3b/crates/context/interface/src/cfg/gas.rs#L16-L25). And on child frame ending, it is send to parent like [this](https://github.com/bluealloy/revm/blob/f1554a6cf0ed8fd25f716eb6eec2a4bbdba9fa3b/crates/handler/src/frame.rs#L566-L567).

author: chfast
content: I noticed this in my impl too. From the first look, this is a nice change.

author: chfast
content: No vacation during 8037 development.

author: chfast
content: This doesn't propose any changes to the charge part: we charge at every instruction and OOG immediately. The proposal is: when you refund/refill state gas (due to REVERT, exceptional halt or negative gas accounting) you refund it to the sources (state_gas_reservoir, regular_gas) where you charged it from.

author: chfast
content: I know this is implementable at frame end (because you also have frame start values). Not sure for instruction level yet.

author: misilva73
content: I think if you agree that implementation-wise this is ok, then I would also support this change. I think it would simplify the revert edge case and avoids the problem of moving gas from gas left to the reservoir

author: draganrakita
content: but there is no revert edge case here

author: draganrakita
content: This new proposal basically moved gas from child state gas to parent gas left, and this is strange

author: draganrakita
content: Impl wise, it is small change for me, so the question is what is more "correct" here. I dont have strong opinion, but lean on putting it in parent reservoir.

author: chfast
content: Only in the case where the child state gas was created out of nowhere (by spill refund). The full proposal (at instruction level) it removes the regular→state gas transmutation effect.

author: draganrakita
content: wdym by removes regular→state gas, if the sstore created the state, state gas should be incremented but regular gas could be consumed, I am not sure if we can remove this link.

author: chfast
content: true. but when the state increase is later reverted, you get back state gas although you spent regular gas. e.g. you can get more state gas out of a call than you started with.

author: draganrakita
content: I guess if call REVERTs and i only had regular gas, it would feel strange this is now part of reservoir.

author: chfast
content: this is current behavior. and the proposal fixes it.

author: draganrakita
content: Yeah i know, but idk what is better tbh. I think as more people lean on fixing it we should do it.

author: chfast
content: you may have issues with this: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2892

author: misilva73
content: This is the problem of moving gas between gas left to the reservoir. I think returning the gas to the place where it was taken from is the most correct (and solves that REVERT edge case where depending on where you revert, you get different gas used). So, if implementation is not a big overhead, I think we should do this

author: draganrakita
content: I dont see a edge case here, where ever you place REVERT, it will always be different gas. Edge case would be on halt as halt can happen inside inside instruction. And amount of state gas that was spend before REVERT with new proposal will be added back to gas_used.

author: garyrong
content: No, it's not at the end of the call. Instead, we charge the state gas when account creation happens. For example, in Call, we charge the state gas when we perform value transfer against the empty recipient

author: draganrakita
content: I see so you want to move it inside calle frame, and reduce callee gas if creation happens?

author: garyrong
content: Yes

author: garyrong
content:
Currently, we pre-charge the state gas beforehand, e.g., in the IntrinsicGas, before the child frame, and refund the pre-charged one if the account creation doesn't happen (e.g, reverted).

What I am proposing is: we charge the state gas in place at where it happens. If the frame is reverted/halted, we refund the state gas by using the Revert/Halt rules

author: garyrong
content: It fixes several things, like in the creation-tx, we blindly pre-charge the account-creation, but it's wrong. The destination can be pre-existing (with non-zero balance) and deployment is allowed by protocol. In this case, the pre-charged state gas **won't be refunded**.

author: garyrong
content: Apart from that, it get rids of crossing-frame charge/revert mechanism. If the frame is reverted, we undo all the changes and refund the state gas with an uniform way, it simplifies the implementation

author: garyrong
content:
Btw, there are two ongoing topics, we should probably create two threads respectively.

a) relocate the account-creation state gas into the child frame
b) reservior-based refund mechanism vs source-based refund mechanism

author: misilva73
content: a) relocate the account-creation state gas into the child frame

author: misilva73
content: b) reservior-based refund mechanism vs source-based refund mechanism
